official_functions.role: Name the packet id assigned in Play clientbound state
Construct, write, and decode the boss_event remove-operation packet body
Define the UUID, operation enum, and empty remove-operation body shape
Write and dispatch-decode the full official Play clientbound boss_event frame

invariants: The official Play clientbound table assigns boss_event immediately after block_update.
The official Play clientbound protocol codec dispatches the boss_event id to ClientboundBossEventPacket.
The official boss_event remove-operation body is UUID followed by operation enum, with no additional remove payload.
The fixture uses official ClientboundBossEventPacket.createRemovePacket(UUID), so it does not require an initialized BossEvent, Level, or game state.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded UUID, decoded operation, Play clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.
This contract does not prove add/update boss-bar payloads, boss-bar UI behavior, entity/world state, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer add/update boss-bar payloads, boss-bar UI behavior, entity/world state, initialized game state, spawn readiness, world load, render readiness, or client-load completion.
